ctxMarket: Attention is on Solana’s growing onchain trading infrastructure, with major exchanges and new communities expanding access. Liquidity movements and DeFi app shutdowns are also drawing scrutiny.
ctxTalking:
• Kraken now offers Solana DEX trading directly in its main app, with plans to add more networks.
• A $1B USDC mint on Solana is coinciding with a DeFi app shutdown, leaving some users in limbo over recovery timelines.
• Frontier Traders launches as a new Solana-based trading community, promoting campaigns and events.
ctxMatters: Solana’s integration into mainstream platforms like Kraken could broaden user access to its ecosystem. Rapid liquidity events, such as the USDC mint, highlight both the network’s scale and the challenges of DeFi app reliability. New trading communities may drive further engagement and activity on Solana.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Major exchanges and new communities are prioritizing Solana for onchain trading. Liquidity flows are outpacing some DeFi recovery systems.
Noise: Speculation around the long-term impact of the recent USDC mint without supporting data.
ctxGlossary:
DEX — Decentralized exchange, enabling peer-to-peer crypto trading without intermediaries.
USDC — A widely used U.S. dollar-pegged stablecoin.

readMore.title: Kraken integrates Solana DEX trading into core app, with support for additional networks planned
Solana’s $1B USDC mint collides with DeFi app shutdown as users face unfinished Drift recovery
